original_abstract: Abstract With the latest advances in deep learning-based generative models, it has not taken long to take advantage of their remarkable performance in the area of time series. Deep neural networks used to work with time series heavily depend on the size and consistency of the datasets used in training. These features are not usually abundant in the real world, where they are usually limited and often have constraints that must be guaranteed. Therefore, an effective way to increase the amount of data is by using data augmentation techniques, either by adding noise or permutations and by generating new synthetic data. This work systematically reviews the current state of the art in the area to provide an overview of all available algorithms and proposes a taxonomy of the most relevant research. The efficiency of the different variants will be evaluated as a central part of the process, as well as the different metrics to evaluate the performance and the main problems concerning each model will be analysed. The ultimate aim of this study is to provide a summary of the evolution and performance of areas that produce better results to guide future researchers in this field.
humanized_abstract: Abstract Deep learning generative models recently gain tremendous advancements in the fields of time series. The generative models using deep neural networks for working with time series data heavily rely on the amount and quality of the training data. Training data for time series is usually limited and have certain constraints that need to be satisfied. Thus, augmenting the amount of training data is a necessity. This can be done in three ways, namely by introducing noise to the data, by introducing certain permutations in the data and by generating new synthetic data. In this manuscript, we present a comprehensive literature review to summarize the current state of the art in this domain. All existing works are summarized and categorized in a taxonomy that we have developed for this purpose. In addition, the performance of the different works are evaluated and the evaluation criteria are highlighted. The most encountered challenges are discussed. The main aim is to provide a summary of the generated time series models that can guide researchers with the advancement of the better performing models.
